// C3: Physical Sensing Components
instance CranePositionEncoder : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Encoder sensor capturing crane position data including trolley and bridge coordinates in Cartesian space for real-time kinematic validation."
    DTDFVocab:producedData CranePositionData
]

instance CraneVelocitySensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Velocity sensor measuring trolley and bridge movement speeds for dynamic validation of digital twin kinematic models."
    DTDFVocab:producedData CraneVelocityData
]

instance CraneAccelerationSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Acceleration sensor capturing jerk and dynamic response data for validating physics-based simulation models."
    DTDFVocab:producedData CraneAccelerationData
]

instance CraneLoadCell : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Load cell measuring payload weight and tension forces for validating digital twin dynamic models and fault detection."
    DTDFVocab:producedData CraneLoadData
]

instance CraneControllerInterface : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Interface capturing controller output signals including setpoints, PID control efforts, and operational status flags."
    DTDFVocab:producedData ControllerCommandData
]

instance RaspberryPiAirQualitySensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Raspberry Pi-based environmental sensor suite for real-time air quality monitoring including CO2, particulate matter, and humidity levels."
    DTDFVocab:producedData AirQualityData
]

instance InfluxDBSensorData : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "InfluxDB integration point for historical IoT sensor data storage and retrieval for greenhouse environmental monitoring."
    DTDFVocab:producedData HistoricalEnvironmentalData
]

// C4: Physical-to-Virtual Interaction
instance CranePositionData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom CranePositionEncoder
    base:desc "Real-time position data transmitted from crane encoders to digital twin for synchronization."
]

instance CraneVelocityData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom CraneVelocitySensor
    base:desc "Real-time velocity data transmitted from crane velocity sensors to digital twin for dynamic validation."
]

instance CraneAccelerationData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom CraneAccelerationSensor
    base:desc "Real-time acceleration data transmitted from crane acceleration sensors to digital twin for physics validation."
]

instance CraneLoadData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom CraneLoadCell
    base:desc "Real-time load data transmitted from crane load cells to digital twin for dynamic model validation."
]

instance ControllerCommandData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom CraneControllerInterface
    base:desc "Controller command data transmitted from physical crane controller to digital twin for bidirectional control validation."
]

instance TrajectoryExecutionData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom CranePositionEncoder, CraneVelocitySensor
    base:desc "Actual movement trajectories including residual swinging motion and end-effector states logged for validation."
]

instance AirQualityData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom RaspberryPiAirQualitySensor
    base:desc "Real-time air quality metrics transmitted from Raspberry Pi sensors to digital twin for indoor environment simulation."
]

instance HistoricalEnvironmentalData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom InfluxDBSensorData
    base:desc "Historical environmental data retrieved from InfluxDB for greenhouse digital twin initialization and model training."
]

// C10: Models/Data
instance CraneKinematicModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Forward/inverse kinematic model defining Cartesian-to-joint transformations for crane motion simulation using MATLAB/Simulink or Python (PyBullet)."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo KinematicSimulationEnabler
]

instance CraneDynamicModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Physics-based dynamic model simulating rigid-body dynamics, friction, and payload effects using Simulink or Gazebo."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo PhysicsSimulationEnabler
]

instance AirQualitySimulationModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "AutomationML-based digital twin model for indoor air quality simulation and virus spread prediction using model-driven engineering methodologies."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo AirQualitySimulationEnabler
]

instance BehavioralAdaptationModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Dynamic behavioral adaptation model for greenhouse climate control using prerecorded data embedded in virtual machine images."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo GreenhouseControlEnabler
]

instance PreRecordedGreenhouseData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Prerecorded environmental data embedded in virtual machine images for greenhouse digital twin initialization and testing."
    DTDFVocab:fromData HistoricalEnvironmentalData
    DTDFVocab:inputTo GreenhouseControlEnabler
]

instance CraneSensorData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Aggregated sensor data including position, velocity, acceleration, and load measurements for crane digital twin synchronization."
    DTDFVocab:fromData CranePositionData, CraneVelocityData, CraneAccelerationData, CraneLoadData
    DTDFVocab:inputTo ContinuousValidationEnabler
]

instance TrajectoryData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Actual trajectory execution data including residual motion and end-effector states for continuous validation against simulated trajectories."
    DTDFVocab:fromData TrajectoryExecutionData
    DTDFVocab:inputTo ContinuousValidationEnabler
]

instance AirQualityMetrics : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Processed air quality metrics including CO2 levels, particulate matter concentrations, and humidity for runtime visualization."
    DTDFVocab:fromData AirQualityData
    DTDFVocab:inputTo AirQualityVisualizationEnabler
]

// C11: Enablers
instance KinematicSimulationEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "MATLAB/Simulink or Python (PyBullet) environment for executing crane kinematic models and generating motion profiles."
    DTDFVocab:enables OptimalControlService, ContinuousValidationService
]

]

instance AirQualitySimulationEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [

]

instance GreenhouseControlEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [

    DTDFVocab:enables GreenhouseBehavioralAdaptationService
]

instance ContinuousValidationEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Custom MATLAB/Python validation framework implementing online validation metrics (RMSE, cross-correlation) for real-time synchronization."
    DTDFVocab:enables ContinuousValidationService
]

instance TimescaleDBEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "TimescaleDB time-series database for structured storage and querying of crane telemetry data with millisecond resolution."
    DTDFVocab:enables HistoricalDataLoggingService
]

instance MQTTBrokerEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "MQTT broker facilitating real-time signaling between crane services including validation alerts and operator notifications."
    DTDFVocab:enables ContinuousValidationService, FaultDetectionService
]

instance AirQualityVisualizationEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Python (Dash/Plotly) or MATLAB App Designer environment for real-time 3D/2D visualization of air quality metrics."
    DTDFVocab:enables AirQualityVisualizationService
]

// C6: Services
instance OptimalControlService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Real-time control optimization service generating jerk-limited trajectories to minimize energy consumption or maximize throughput."

]

]

]

instance GreenhouseBehavioralAdaptationService : DTDFVocab:Service [

]

instance virtual_to_physical_interaction : DTDFVocab:VirtualToPhysical [
    base:desc "
The **virtual-to-physical interaction** in the Digital Twin system is mediated by the **Control Application**, which translates digital commands into physical actions for the **Physical Crane**. Key aspects include:

**Command Types**:
- **Trajectory commands**: Generated by the **Trajectory Generator Service** using kinematic models to produce optimal movement paths (e.g., swing-free trajectories for puck/container transport).
- **Manual overrides**: Issued via the **Human Machine Interface** (e.g., emergency stops, position adjustments).

**Control Message Formats**:
- Not explicitly detailed, but inferred to be **structured data objects** (e.g., JSON or protobuf) transmitted via the **Physical Crane Interface** to the crane’s controllers.
- Commands likely include:
  - Target coordinates (X/Y/Z positions).
  - Velocity/acceleration profiles.
  - Operational modes (e.g., automatic/semi-automatic).

**Validation and Authentication Mechanisms**:
- **Continuous Validation Service**: Compares executed trajectories (physical) with simulated trajectories (virtual) using **distance metrics** (e.g., Euclidean error) against predefined thresholds. If breaches occur, operators are alerted via the **Dashboard**.
- **No explicit authentication** is mentioned, but the **Control Application**’s role as a Python intermediary suggests implicit trust (e.g., no cryptographic verification of commands).

**Feedback Systems**:
- The **Logging Application** captures real-time crane state (e.g., position, velocity) and feeds it back to the **Digital Twin** for validation.
- The **Dashboard** provides visual feedback (e.g., trajectory plots, error alerts) to operators.

**Conflict Resolution**:
- Not explicitly detailed, but the **Control Application** likely prioritizes:
  - **Trajectory Generator Service** commands (optimized paths) over manual overrides, with fallback to operator intervention if validation fails.
  - **Emergency stops** (via HMI) override all other commands.

**Command Execution Confirmation**:
- Implicit via the **Continuous Validation Service**, which verifies whether the physical crane followed the intended trajectory. Confirmed execution is inferred if the distance metric remains within thresholds.
"
]

instance dt_constellation : DTDFVocab:Constellation [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin systems in the documents follow an **ad-hoc, component-based orchestration** rather than a standardized framework. Key patterns include:
  - **Modular microservices**: The **GreenhouseDT** uses **InfluxDB (time-series database)**, **ActiveMQ (message broker)**, and **virtual machines** to decouple data storage, messaging, and execution environments. The **Air Quality Management** exemplar employs **AutomationML** for model-driven engineering and **Microsoft Azure tools** for pipeline orchestration, indicating a **hybrid cloud-edge architecture**.
  - **Containerization/virtualization**: All exemplars leverage **virtual machines (GreenhouseDT)** or **containerized deployment** (implied by the discussion section) for reproducibility and scalability.
  - **Event-driven coordination**: The use of **message brokers (ActiveMQ)** and **cloud services (Azure)** suggests **asynchronous, event-based integration** between physical sensors, digital twin models, and external systems.
  - **Domain-specific adaptation**: The **GreenhouseDT** focuses on **self-adaptive behavioral synchronization** between the physical greenhouse and its digital twin, while the **Air Quality Management** twin prioritizes **runtime visualization, simulation, and prediction** via AutomationML models. The **gantry crane** (though undetailed) aligns with **cyber-physical system (CPS) orchestration**, likely using **C4 Model-based architecture** for software-physical system integration.
  The absence of a **commercial digital twin framework** (e.g., Siemens MindSphere, PTC ThingWorx) implies **custom orchestration logic**, with **resource management** handled via **cloud-edge hybrid deployment** (Azure) or **local virtualization** (VMs)."
]

    base:desc "The **GreenhouseDT** exemplar emphasizes **self-adaptive behavioral models**, suggesting dynamic fidelity adjustments to align the digital twin with the physical system’s evolving state. Validation methods are not explicitly described, but the use of **prerecorded data** in virtual machines implies **offline testing** and reproducibility. The **Air Quality Management** exemplar focuses on **runtime validation** through **visualization, simulation, and prediction**, with no mention of formal verification protocols (e.g., model checking, uncertainty quantification). Error detection/correction mechanisms are absent, though the **ad-hoc pipeline** (e.g., Azure tools) may include basic anomaly detection via sensor data analysis. Quality assurance processes are implied through **containerization** (ensuring reproducibility) but lack technical specifics (e.g., calibration procedures, traceability standards)."
]

instance dt_technical_connection : DTDFVocab:TechnicalConnection [
    base:desc "
The **Digital Twin’s technical network infrastructure** is designed to support real-time and asynchronous communication between its six containers. Key details include:

**Communication Protocols**:
- **MQTT**: Used for lightweight, publish-subscribe messaging between services (e.g., **Trajectory Generator Service** → **Simulation Service**, **Continuous Validation Service** → **Dashboard**).
- **Database Protocol**: **TimescaleDB** (PostgreSQL-compatible) for structured time-series data storage, accessed via standard SQL or proprietary APIs.
- **Physical Interface Protocol**: Undisclosed, but likely a **serial/TCP/IP** link between the **Physical Crane Interface** (Python) and crane controllers.

**Network Topology and Architecture**:
- **Centralized**: Services communicate via the **MQTT Broker**, acting as a hub for event-driven interactions.
- **Containerized Services**: Deployed as independent Python applications, with the **Database** as a shared resource.
- **No explicit cloud/edge distinction**: Assumed to be **on-premise** due to the lab-scale, real-time control requirements.

**Bandwidth and Latency Requirements**:
- **Low-latency**: Critical for trajectory validation (e.g., **Continuous Validation Service** requires near-real-time comparison of executed vs. simulated trajectories).
- **Bandwidth**: Moderate, primarily for:
  - Streaming crane state data (e.g., position/velocity) to **TimescaleDB**.
  - MQTT messages (e.g., validation alerts, trajectory updates).

**Reliability and Redundancy Mechanisms**:
- **Database Replication**: Not explicitly mentioned, but **TimescaleDB** supports high availability configurations.
- **Service Resilience**: The **Logging Application** and **Control Application** are likely designed for fault tolerance (e.g., retries on failed controller commands).
- **No explicit redundancy** for the **Physical Crane Interface** or **MQTT Broker**, but the system can operate without the Digital Twin (implying graceful degradation).

**Security Protocols**:
- **Not in Document**: No details on encryption (e.g., TLS for MQTT), access control, or network segmentation are provided.
"
]

instance dt_hosting_deployment : DTDFVocab:Deployment [
    base:desc "
The **Digital Twin’s hosting and deployment infrastructure** is tailored for a lab-scale gantry crane system with real-time control and simulation requirements. Key aspects include:

**Hosting Platforms**:
- **On-premise**: All containers (e.g., **Database**, **Trajectory Generator Service**, **Simulation Service**) are deployed locally to minimize latency for real-time operations.
- **No cloud/edge hybrid**: The system’s critical path (e.g., trajectory validation) necessitates co-location with the **Physical Crane**.

**Computational and Storage Requirements**:
- **Database**: **TimescaleDB** (PostgreSQL-based) for time-series data storage, with requirements dependent on:
  - Logging frequency (continuous crane state updates).
  - Historical trajectory data retention.
- **Services**:
  - **Trajectory Generator Service**: Requires computational resources for kinematic model processing (e.g., solving differential equations for optimal paths).
  - **Simulation Service**: Demands sufficient CPU/GPU to model crane dynamics in real-time.
  - **MQTT Broker**: Lightweight, but must handle high-frequency messages (e.g., validation alerts).
- **No explicit hardware specs**: Assumed to be standard x86 servers or high-performance workstations.

**Scalability Mechanisms**:
- **Vertical Scaling**: Services (e.g., **Trajectory Generator Service**) can be scaled by allocating more CPU/memory to handle complex trajectories.
- **No horizontal scaling**: The lab-scale system’s simplicity precludes distributed deployment (e.g., no Kubernetes clusters or microservice orchestration).
- **Database Scaling**: **TimescaleDB** supports partitioning and sharding for large datasets, though not explicitly configured here.

**Deployment Models Supported**:
- **Monolithic-like**: Services are containerized (per C4 model) but deployed as standalone Python applications, not container orchestration (e.g., Docker Swarm).
- **Manual Deployment**: Likely managed via scripts or configuration files (e.g., Python `requirements.txt`, service-specific configs).

**Availability/Reliability Assurance**:
- **High Availability**: Implied for critical services (e.g., **Logging Application** must not lose data; **Control Application** must not drop commands).
- **Redundancy**: Not explicitly detailed, but the system’s ability to operate **without the Digital Twin** suggests resilience to Digital Twin failures.
- **Backup/Recovery**: **TimescaleDB** likely includes point-in-time recovery for data integrity.
"
]

instance horizontal_integration : DTDFVocab:HoriIntegration [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin systems demonstrate **limited but critical horizontal integration** with external systems, primarily through:
  - **Data exchange protocols**:
    - **GreenhouseDT**: Uses **ActiveMQ (message broker)** for inter-component communication, with **InfluxDB** as the central data repository. No explicit interoperability standards (e.g., OPC UA, MQTT) are mentioned, but the use of **virtual machines with prerecorded data** suggests **batch or periodic synchronization** with external systems.
    - **Air Quality Management**: Relies on **AutomationML** for model exchange and **Microsoft Azure tools** for cloud-based pipeline integration, implying compliance with **industry standards for construction engineering** (e.g., IFC for building data). The **Raspberry Pi sensors** likely interface with the digital twin via **custom APIs or proprietary protocols**, with no mention of standardized IoT protocols (e.g., MQTT, CoAP).
  - **Dependencies**:
    - **GreenhouseDT**: Depends on **InfluxDB (open-source)** and **ActiveMQ (open-source)**, reducing vendor lock-in but requiring manual integration with external systems.
    - **Air Quality Management**: Has a **strong dependency on Microsoft Azure**, which may enforce **Azure-specific integration patterns** (e.g., Azure IoT Hub, Event Grid) for external system connectivity.
  - **Integration patterns**:
    - **GreenhouseDT**: Adopts a **loosely coupled** architecture via message brokers, enabling **plug-and-play** extensions (e.g., adding new sensors or actuators).
    - **Air Quality Management**: Uses a **pipeline-based approach** (Azure tools) for **runtime visualization, simulation, and prediction**, suggesting **modular integration** with external analytics or ERP systems via **cloud APIs**.
    - **Gantry Crane**: Though undetailed, the mention of **C4 Model** implies **software-centric integration**, likely using **REST/gRPC APIs** for inter-system communication. The **cyber-physical focus** suggests integration with **logistics/warehouse management systems (WMS)** or **industrial control systems (ICS)**.
  - **Interoperability standards**: Only **AutomationML** is explicitly cited (Air Quality Management), indicating **domain-specific standardization** for construction engineering. No broader standards (e.g., OPC UA, FIWARE) are documented."
]

instance standardization : DTDFVocab:Standardization [
    base:desc "The **Air Quality Management** exemplar adheres to **AutomationML** for digital twin modeling, a standardized format for engineering data exchange. The **GreenhouseDT** exemplar uses **InfluxDB** (a time-series database standard) and **ActiveMQ** (a widely adopted message broker protocol), but no formal industry standards or certifications are mentioned. The **lab-scale gantry crane** exemplar does not reference any standardization frameworks. **Interoperability** is implied through the use of **containerization/virtualization** (e.g., VMs, Raspberry Pi) and **GitHub-hosted repositories**, but no compliance frameworks (e.g., ISO, IEC) are explicitly adopted."
]
